title: 1.1: Tests that buildQueryTransaction calculates an appropriate time interval when given a large time range with standard data point limits and minimum interval constraints
1.2: Tests that buildQueryTransaction uses the minimum interval threshold when it takes precedence over a calculated interval from a small time range
1.3: Tests that buildQueryTransaction calculates interval based on maxDataPoints constraints when they are more restrictive than the time range would normally suggest
1.4: Tests that buildQueryTransaction returns a request with skipQueryCache set to true
2.1: Places the test for large time range interval calculation in public/app/core/utils/explore.test.ts
2.2: Places the test for minInterval precedence in public/app/core/utils/explore.test.ts
2.3: Places the test for maxDataPoints constraint in public/app/core/utils/explore.test.ts
2.4: Places the test for query cache configuration in public/app/core/utils/explore.test.ts
3.1: All tests use Jest framework with describe and it blocks and expect assertions
3.2: All tests follow a naming pattern that describes the expected behavior
3.3: All tests use Arrange-Act-Assert structure with variable setup, function invocation, and expect assertion phases
4.1: All tests create time range objects with from and to properties representing DateTime values and a raw property for the original range specification
4.2: All tests group buildQueryTransaction tests within a describe block
